topNews.title: Show HN: My first app, artisanally vibe-coded in 4 months
topNews.rawTextSnippet: Hi HN, I'm Jeroen, 49. Father of two, and manager in the Dutch government by day. Until last December I had never written a line of code. You get the picture. Since April my first app, TravElly, is live in the App Store. Normally during Christmas I tend to build something by hand. Last year I s…
topNews.summary:
• What happened: Jeroen launched his first app, TravElly, after four months of self-taught coding.  
• Why it matters: This showcases the potential for individuals to create viable tech products regardless of their background.  
• Business opportunity: There is a growing market for user-friendly apps developed by non-professionals, appealing to niche audiences.

topNews.title: Show HN: Kanban CLI (A local-first, agent-first task manager for the terminal)
topNews.rawTextSnippet: Hello HN, Ever since agents have become increasingly common in development, I've been scratching my head as to how to control their randomness. Recently, I decided to emulate an issue-tracking and project-management tool for agent-driven workflows. Kanban is a Rust-based coordination layer desi…
topNews.summary:
• What happened: A new task manager called Kanban CLI has been introduced for terminal use.  
• Why it matters: It addresses the need for better control over randomness in agent-driven workflows.  
• Business opportunity: There is potential for integration with existing development tools to enhance productivity in project management.

• Business opportunity: Companies can develop solutions to mitigate these security risks, creating a demand for enhanced supply chain security tools.

topNews.title: Local LLMs perform better when you teach them to ask before they answer

• Business opportunity: Companies can develop and market LLMs that incorporate this questioning technique for better user engagement and satisfaction.

research.title: Understanding Data Temporality Impact on Large Language Models Pre-training
research.rawTextSnippet: Large language models (LLMs) are typically trained on shuffled corpora, yielding models whose knowledge is frozen at train time and whose temporal grounding remains poorly understood. research.rawTextSnippet: Shapley and Banzhaf interactions capture the complex dynamics inherent in modern machine learning applications. funding.title: How VCs and founders use inflated ‘ARR’ to crown AI startups
funding.rawTextSnippet: Some AI startups are stretching traditional revenue metrics when talking about progress publicly. And their investors are fully aware.
funding.summary:
• What happened: Some AI startups are inflating their Annual Recurring Revenue (ARR) figures to present a more favorable view of their progress.  
• Why it matters: This practice can mislead investors and distort the true financial health of these companies.  
• Business opportunity: There is potential for creating transparent financial assessment tools to help investors evaluate startup metrics accurately.

funding.title: Elon Musk can’t hear you over the sound of his $1.75 trillion IPO
funding.rawTextSnippet: The SpaceX S-1 is finally here, and the story it tells goes way further than rockets. The filing runs to 36 pages of risk factors alone, and the numbers inside match the ambition: a $28 trillion total addressable market, a pay package tied to establishing a Mars colony,…

dailyBrief: In a revealing account, Greg Brockman detailed the tumultuous 72 hours that nearly jeopardized OpenAI's future, highlighting the intense pressures and strategic decisions that shape AI leadership. Meanwhile, Project Glasswing has provided an initial update, signaling advancements in AI-driven tools that could enhance productivity across industries. On the grassroots level, developers showcased innovative projects like a local-first task manager and a unique app, emphasizing the growing democratization of AI technology. Additionally, a new analysis underscores that the semiconductor supply chain for AI is a complex web involving 30 countries, suggesting that businesses must navigate geopolitical tensions and supply chain vulnerabilities to secure their AI infrastructure. These developments indicate that both strategic leadership and grassroots innovation will be crucial for companies looking to leverage AI effectively in a competitive landscape.
